title: papaver armeniacum var. persicum
intro: Papaver armeniacum var. persicum, also known as Armenian poppy or Persian poppy, is a flowering plant in the poppy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Turkey. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Papaver armeniacum var. persicum include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and calm the nervous system, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. The plant is often prepared as a dried herb or used in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
